action: Integrate exact repository-reference verification with deterministic queue candidate materialization and preserve invalid updates in a separate report.
selection_basis: M-013 identified integration of queue-reference-verifier.mjs with queue-state-materializer.mjs as the next highest-value continuity action.
memory_recovered: Queue reconstruction is a gated pipeline, not two independent utilities: verify each update against an authenticated immutable inventory, exclude invalid references without erasing their evidence, materialize only accepted updates, and require review before canonical replacement.
contradictions_resolved: A deterministic materializer can still produce an ungrounded candidate when reference verification is not part of the same pipeline.
Excluding an invalid update from candidate state must not delete or hide the rejected event; it belongs in a retained invalid-reference report.
Repository-reference verification raises provenance confidence but does not establish semantic truth, deployment, test execution, peer execution, or human outcome.

observed: The integrated candidate builder and five-test contract are committed.
Five deterministic tests passed in an isolated Node runtime.
No canonical queue, registry, trigger ledger, prior team output, file, or Git history was overwritten or deleted.
inferred: With a trustworthy immutable inventory, invalid output bindings cannot enter the generated candidate queue through this builder.
proposed: Enumerate all append-only queue updates and immutable commit/path bindings, then generate and retain the first repository-wide candidate package.
superseded: Materializing queue updates before or separately from exact reference verification.
unresolved: Repository-wide candidate generation and explicit human review.

verification: 5 Node tests passed: accepted update materialization, missing-commit exclusion, mixed validity separation, inventory cardinality fail-closed behavior, and claim-ceiling enforcement.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract queue semantics only; no raw private chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.
canonical_queue_update_policy: Append-only team-owned update; operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json was not overwritten.
next_action: In an authenticated checkout, enumerate every operations/queue-updates record and exact commit/path binding, run build-verified-queue-candidate.mjs, and retain candidate-effective-queue.json, verification report, invalid-reference report, conflict report, and review record.
